risinfo.chaptertitle: 18. Common Surgical Procedures and Sedation in Facility Settings

sect1.title: 27713Osteotomy(ies), tibia, including fibula when performed, unilateral, with insertion of an externally controlled intramedullary lengthening device, including imaging, alignment assessments, computations of adjustment schedules, and management of the intramedullary lengthening device
sect1.para: Note that codes 27458 and 27713 report unilateral procedures that are performed to correct a limb length discrepancy (eg, Q72.812, congenital shortening of left lower limb).
× Do not report 27458 in conjunction with other osteotomy procedures (27450, 27466) or fracture treatment (27470, 27472, 27506).
× Do not report 27713 in conjunction with other osteotomy procedures (27705, 27709, 27712) osteoplasty (27715), or fracture treatment (27720, 27722, 27724, 27759).
Code 27466 (osteoplasty, femur; combined lengthening and shortening with femoral segment transfer) is deleted in 2026. This procedure is no longer commonly performed.

sect4.title: Removal of Musculoskeletal Hardware
sect4.para: An object intentionally placed by a physician or other QHP for any purpose (eg, diagnostic, therapeutic) is considered an implant. If an implant (or part thereof) has moved from its original position or is structurally broken and no longer serves its intended purpose or presents a hazard to the patient, it qualifies as a foreign body for coding purposes, unless CPT coding instructions direct otherwise or a specific CPT code exists to describe the removal of that broken/moved implant.
20665 Removal of tongs or halo applied by another individual 20670 Removal of implant; superficial (eg, buried wire, pin or rod) (separate procedure)

20680 deep (eg, buried pin, screw, metal band, nail, rod or plate) Removal of a halo or tongs is included in the placement procedure. Removal of a halo or tongs by another physician, however, is separately reported with 20665. (Note: Physicians of the same specialty and same group practice are typically considered the same physician for billing purposes.)

Codes 20670 and 20680 are reported for removal of orthopedic hardware. If it is necessary to remove an implant within the global period (eg, to address breakage), report the appropriate removal code with modifier 78 (unplanned return to the OR by the same physician or other QHP following initial procedure for a related procedure during the postoperative period). Removal of an external fixation system under anesthesia (20694) requires more than local anesthesia.

sect2.title: Intubation and Airway Management

listitem.para: Moderate sedation (99151–99157) may be reported in addition to the intubation if used to sedate the patient before and during the intubation procedure (eg, in rapid sequence intubation) and reporting criteria are met. Refer to the Sedation section later in this chapter for more on moderate sedation.

Endotracheal intubation is included in the neonatal and pediatric critical care services reported with 99468–99476 and intensive care services reported with 99477–99480 and should not be billed separately. Code 31500 is separately reported for endotracheal intubation performed as part of neonatal resuscitation in the delivery room (ie, before admission).

sect2.para: 31502 Tracheostomy tube change prior to establishment of fistula tract Report 31502 only when an indwelling tracheostomy tube is replaced before establishment of a fistula tract.

Routine changing of a tracheostomy tube after a fistula tract has been established is included in a related E/M service. Code 31575 (laryngoscopy, flexible; diagnostic) may be reported when performed to check tracheostomy tube placement.

31505–31520 Indirect laryngoscopy Report 31505 for indirect diagnostic laryngoscopy. If, while performing this procedure, a foreign body is removed, report 31511 (laryngoscopy with removal of foreign body).
31515 Direct laryngoscopy with or without tracheoscopy Code 31515 is reported when laryngoscopy is performed for aspiration.
31520 Laryngoscopy direct, with or without tracheoscopy; diagnostic, newborn Do not report modifier 63 (procedure performed on infant <4 kg) in conjunction with 31520.
31525 Laryngoscopy, direct, with or without tracheoscopy; diagnostic except newborn

sect4.title: Thoracostomy, Thoracentesis, and Pleural Drainage
sect4.para: 32551 Tube thoracostomy, includes connection to drainage system (eg, water seal), when performed, open (separate procedure) 32554 Thoracentesis, needle or catheter, aspiration of the pleural space; without imaging guidance
32555 with imaging guidance 32556 Pleural drainage, percutaneous, with insertion of indwelling catheter; without imaging guidance

listitem.para: Thoracostomy (32551) represents an open procedure involving incision into a rib interspace with dissection extending through the chest wall muscles and pleura. Thoracentesis and percutaneous pleural drainage may be more commonly performed in pediatrics except for neonatal and pediatric critical care.

sect4.para: ✱ Percutaneous image guidance cannot be reported in conjunction with 32551, which represents an open procedure. Diagnostic ultrasonography performed before thoracostomy to localize a collection in the pleural space is separately reportable only when the complete ultrasound study of the chest (76604) is performed with permanent recording of images.

listitem.para: Thoracentesis codes 32554 and 32555 represent a procedure in which the surgical technique is puncture of the pleural space to remove fluid or air. Although a catheter (eg, pigtail catheter) may be used, it is not left in place at the end of the procedure.

Percutaneous pleural drainage codes 32556 and 32557 represent procedures in which an indwelling catheter is inserted into the pleural space and remains at the end of the procedure. The catheter is secured in place and connected to a suction drainage system.
Removal of chest tubes and non-tunneled indwelling pleural catheters is included in a related E/M service and not in a separately reportable procedure.
For insertion of a tunneled pleural catheter with cuff, refer to 32550. For removal of a tunneled pleural catheter with cuff, refer to 32552.
